    12→#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider

    14→#   - claude_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - DEPRECATED: Use llm_invoke
    15→#   - claude_extract_json(output) - DEPRECATED: Use llm_extract_json

    23→

    25→# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE

    28→#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
    29→#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
    30→#   - gemini-cli: Google Gemini CLI
    31→#   - custom: User-defined command template
    32→#
    33→# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
    34→# ============================================================================
    35→
    36→# Find Claude CLI binary
    37→# DEPRECATED: Use llm_find_bin("claude-cli") instead
    38→# Returns:
    39→#   Path to claude binary
    40→# Exits:
    41→#   3 if claude binary not found
    42→claude_find_bin() {
    43→    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    44→    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
    45→        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
    46→        return 0
    47→    fi
    48→
    49→    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    50→    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    51→    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
    52→        echo "$local_claude"
    53→        return 0
    54→    fi
    55→
    56→    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    57→    local path_claude
    58→    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)

    60→        echo "$path_claude"

    62→    fi
    63→
    64→    # Not found
    65→    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    66→    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2

    68→}
    69→
    70→# Find LLM binary for specified provider
    71→# Args:
    72→#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, gemini-cli, custom)
    73→# Returns:
    74→#   Path to binary
    75→# Exits:
    76→#   3 if binary not found
    77→llm_find_bin() {
    78→    local provider="$1"

    80→    local config_var=""
    81→    local default_name=""
    82→
    83→    case "$provider" in

    89→        gemini-cli)

    91→            default_name="gemini"
    92→            ;;
    93→        custom)
    94→            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"

    97→        openai-api)
    98→            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
    99→            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
   100→                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2

   103→            echo "curl"
   104→            return 0
   105→            ;;
   106→        *)

   139→# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
   140→# Args:
   141→#   $1 - model name
   142→#   $2 - prompt text
   143→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   144→# Returns:
   145→#   Raw output from Claude CLI
   146→_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
   147→    local model="$1"
   148→    local prompt="$2"
   149→    local timeout="$3"
   150→
   151→    local claude_bin
   152→    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")
   153→
   154→    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   155→    local workspace
   156→    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")
   157→
   158→    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
   159→    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"
   160→
   161→    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
   162→    local output
   163→    local claude_errors
   164→    local claude_output_file
   165→    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
   166→    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)
   167→
   168→    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
   169→    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
   170→    set +e
   171→    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
   172→        --model "$model" \
   173→        --setting-sources project \
   174→        <<EOF
   175→$prompt
   176→EOF
   177→    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
   178→    local exit_code=$?
   179→    set -e
   180→
   181→    # Read output from file
   182→    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
   183→    rm -f "$claude_output_file"
   184→
   185→    # Check if Claude invocation failed
   186→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   187→        local errors
   188→        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   189→        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   190→        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
   191→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   192→        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
   193→        rm -f "$claude_errors"
   194→        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   195→        return 1
   196→    fi
   197→
   198→    # Check for stderr warnings
   199→    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
   200→        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
   201→    fi
   202→    rm -f "$claude_errors"
   203→
   204→    # Cleanup workspace
   205→    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   206→
   207→    echo "$output"
   208→}
   209→
   210→# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
   211→# Args:
   212→#   $1 - model name
   213→#   $2 - prompt text
   214→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   215→# Returns:
   216→#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
   217→_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
   218→    local model="$1"
   219→    local prompt="$2"
   220→    local timeout="$3"
   221→
   222→    # Get API key from config or environment
   223→    local api_key
   224→    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
   225→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   226→        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
   227→    fi
   228→
   229→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   230→        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
   231→        return 1
   232→    fi
   233→
   234→    # Get endpoint
   235→    local endpoint
   236→    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
   237→    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}
   238→
   239→    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   240→    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"
   241→
   242→    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
   243→    local escaped_prompt
   244→    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)
   245→
   246→    local payload
   247→    payload=$(cat <<EOF
   248→{
   249→  "model": "$model",
   250→  "messages": [
   251→    {
   252→      "role": "user",
   253→      "content": $escaped_prompt
   254→    }
   255→  ],
   256→  "temperature": 0.7
   257→}
   258→EOF
   259→)
   260→
   261→    # Invoke API with curl
   262→    local output
   263→    local curl_errors
   264→    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
   265→
   266→    set +e
   267→    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
   268→        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   269→        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
   270→        -d "$payload" \
   271→        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
   272→    local exit_code=$?
   273→    set -e
   274→
   275→    # Check for curl errors
   276→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   277→        local errors
   278→        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   279→        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   280→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   281→        rm -f "$curl_errors"
   282→        return 1
   283→    fi
   284→    rm -f "$curl_errors"
   285→
   286→    # Check for API errors in response
   287→    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   288→        local error_msg
   289→        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
   290→        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
   291→        return 1
   292→    fi
   293→
   294→    # Extract content from response
   295→    local content
   296→    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')
   297→
   298→    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
   299→        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
   300→        log_error "Response: $output"
   301→        return 1
   302→    fi
   303→
   304→    echo "$content"
   305→}
   306→
   307→# Internal: Invoke Gemini CLI provider
   308→# Args:
   309→#   $1 - model name
   310→#   $2 - prompt text
   311→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   312→# Returns:
   313→#   Raw output from Gemini CLI
   314→_llm_invoke_gemini_cli() {
   315→    local model="$1"
   316→    local prompt="$2"
   317→    local timeout="$3"
   318→
   319→    local gemini_bin
   320→    gemini_bin=$(llm_find_bin "gemini-cli")
   321→
   322→    log_debug "Invoking Gemini CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   323→    log_debug "Using Gemini binary: ${gemini_bin}"

   325→    # Invoke Gemini CLI
   326→    # NOTE: This is a placeholder implementation - actual Gemini CLI may differ

   328→    local gemini_errors

   330→

   333→        --model "$model" \
   334→        <<EOF
   335→$prompt
   336→EOF
   337→    2>"$gemini_errors")

   339→    set -e
   340→
   341→    # Check for errors
   342→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   343→        local errors
   344→        errors=$(cat "$gemini_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   345→        log_error "Gemini CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   346→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   347→        rm -f "$gemini_errors"
   348→        return 1
   349→    fi
   350→
   351→    # Check for stderr warnings
   352→    if [ -s "$gemini_errors" ]; then
   353→        log_warn "Gemini CLI stderr: $(cat "$gemini_errors")"
   354→    fi
   355→    rm -f "$gemini_errors"
   356→
   357→    echo "$output"
   358→}
   359→
   360→# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
   361→# Args:
   362→#   $1 - model name
   363→#   $2 - prompt text
   364→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   365→# Returns:
   366→#   Raw output from custom command
   367→_llm_invoke_custom() {
   368→    local model="$1"
   369→    local prompt="$2"
   370→    local timeout="$3"
   371→
   372→    local custom_bin
   373→    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")
   374→
   375→    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
   376→        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
   377→        return 1
   378→    fi
   379→
   380→    local command_template
   381→    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")
   382→
   383→    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
   384→        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
   385→        return 1
   386→    fi
   387→
   388→    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
   389→    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
   390→    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"
   391→
   392→    # Write prompt to temporary file
   393→    local prompt_file
   394→    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
   395→    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"
   396→
   397→    # Substitute placeholders in command template
   398→    local command="$command_template"
   399→    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
   400→    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
   401→    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
   402→    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"
   403→
   404→    log_debug "Executing: $command"
   405→
   406→    # Execute command
   407→    local output
   408→    local command_errors
   409→    command_errors=$(mktemp)
   410→
   411→    set +e
   412→    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
   413→    local exit_code=$?
   414→    set -e
   415→
   416→    # Cleanup prompt file
   417→    rm -f "$prompt_file"
   418→
   419→    # Check for errors
   420→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   421→        local errors
   422→        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   423→        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   424→        log_error "Command: $command"
   425→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   426→        rm -f "$command_errors"
   427→        return 1
   428→    fi
   429→
   430→    # Check for stderr warnings
   431→    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
   432→        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
   433→    fi
   434→    rm -f "$command_errors"
   435→
   436→    echo "$output"
   437→}
   438→
   439→# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
   440→# Args:
   441→#   $1 - model name
   442→#   $2 - prompt text
   443→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
   444→# Returns:
   445→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   446→# Exits:
   447→#   1 on error
   448→llm_invoke() {
   449→    local model="$1"
   450→    local prompt="$2"
   451→    local timeout="${3:-30}"
   452→
   453→    # Get provider from config
   454→    local provider
   455→    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
   456→    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}
   457→
   458→    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"
   459→
   460→    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
   461→    local output
   462→    case "$provider" in
   463→        claude-cli)
   464→            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   465→            ;;
   466→        openai-api)
   467→            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   468→            ;;
   469→        gemini-cli)
   470→            output=$(_llm_invoke_gemini_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   471→            ;;
   472→        custom)
   473→            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
   474→            ;;
   475→        *)
   476→            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
   477→            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, gemini-cli, custom"
   478→            return 1
   479→            ;;
   480→    esac
   481→
   482→    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
   483→    local json
   484→    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")
   485→
   486→    # Validate JSON
   487→    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
   488→        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
   489→        return 1
   490→    fi
   491→
   492→    echo "$json"
   493→}
   494→
   495→# DEPRECATED: Use llm_invoke() instead
   496→# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
   497→# Args:
   498→#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
   499→#   $2 - prompt text
   500→#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
   501→# Returns:
   502→#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   503→# Exits:
   504→#   1 on error
   505→claude_invoke() {
   506→    # Backward compatibility wrapper - delegates to llm_invoke
   507→    llm_invoke "$@"
   508→}
   509→
   510→# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
   511→# Args:
   512→#   $1 - LLM output text
   513→# Returns:
   514→#   Extracted JSON
   515→llm_extract_json() {
   516→    local output="$1"
   517→
   518→    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
   519→    local extracted
   520→    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")
   521→
   522→    # If extraction changed the output, use it
   523→    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
   524→        echo "$extracted"
   525→        return 0
   526→    fi
   527→
   528→    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
   529→    # Match from first { to last }
   530→    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
   531→        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
   532→    else
   533→        echo "$output"
   534→    fi
   535→}
   536→
   537→# DEPRECATED: Use llm_extract_json() instead
   538→# Backward compatibility alias
   539→claude_extract_json() {
   540→    llm_extract_json "$@"
   541→}
   542→
   543→# ============================================================================
   544→

#!/bin/bash
# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
#
# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
#   - Claude CLI (default)
#   - OpenAI API
#   - Gemini CLI
#   - Custom command templates
#
# Functions:
#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output
#   - claude_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - DEPRECATED: Use llm_invoke
#   - claude_extract_json(output) - DEPRECATED: Use llm_extract_json
#   - claude_find_bin() - DEPRECATED: Use llm_find_bin("claude-cli")

# Double-source guard
if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then
    return 0
fi
readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1


# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
# ============================================================================
# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
#   - gemini-cli: Google Gemini CLI
#   - custom: User-defined command template
#
# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
# ============================================================================

# Find Claude CLI binary
# DEPRECATED: Use llm_find_bin("claude-cli") instead
# Returns:
#   Path to claude binary
# Exits:
#   3 if claude binary not found
claude_find_bin() {
    # 1. Check CLAUDE_BIN config
    if [ -n "${CLAUDE_BIN:-}" ] && [ -x "$CLAUDE_BIN" ]; then
        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"
        return 0
    fi

    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
        echo "$local_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # 3. Check PATH using command -v
    local path_claude
    path_claude=$(command -v claude 2>/dev/null || true)
    if [ -n "$path_claude" ] && [ -x "$path_claude" ]; then
        echo "$path_claude"
        return 0
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Claude CLI binary not found" >&2
    echo "  Checked: CLAUDE_BIN, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH" >&2
    exit 3
}

# Find LLM binary for specified provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, gemini-cli, custom)
# Returns:
#   Path to binary
# Exits:
#   3 if binary not found
llm_find_bin() {
    local provider="$1"
    local bin_path=""
    local config_var=""
    local default_name=""

    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            # Use existing claude_find_bin logic
            claude_find_bin
            return $?
            ;;
        gemini-cli)
            config_var="LLM_GEMINI_BIN"
            default_name="gemini"
            ;;
        custom)
            config_var="LLM_CUSTOM_BIN"
            default_name="" # No default for custom
            ;;
        openai-api)
            # OpenAI uses curl (no custom binary needed)
            command -v curl >/dev/null 2>&1 || {
                echo "ERROR: curl not found (required for openai-api provider)" >&2
                exit 3
            }
            echo "curl"
            return 0
            ;;
        *)
            echo "ERROR: Unknown LLM provider: $provider" >&2
            return 3
            ;;
    esac

    # Check config variable first
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        bin_path=$(config_get "$config_var")
        if [ -n "$bin_path" ] && [ -x "$bin_path" ]; then
            echo "$bin_path"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Fall back to PATH lookup
    if [ -n "$default_name" ]; then
        local path_bin
        path_bin=$(command -v "$default_name" 2>/dev/null || true)
        if [ -n "$path_bin" ] && [ -x "$path_bin" ]; then
            echo "$path_bin"
            return 0
        fi
    fi

    # Not found
    echo "ERROR: Binary not found for provider $provider" >&2
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        echo "  Checked: $config_var config, PATH" >&2
    fi
    return 3
}

# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Claude CLI
_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local claude_bin
    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")

    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
    local workspace
    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")

    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"

    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
    local output
    local claude_errors
    local claude_output_file
    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)

    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
    set +e
    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
        --model "$model" \
        --setting-sources project \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Read output from file
    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
    rm -f "$claude_output_file"

    # Check if Claude invocation failed
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
        rm -f "$claude_errors"
        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$claude_errors"

    # Cleanup workspace
    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    # Get API key from config or environment
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get endpoint
    local endpoint
    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}

    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"

    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
    local escaped_prompt
    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)

    local payload
    payload=$(cat <<EOF
{
  "model": "$model",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "user",
      "content": $escaped_prompt
    }
  ],
  "temperature": 0.7
}
EOF
)

    # Invoke API with curl
    local output
    local curl_errors
    curl_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
        -d "$payload" \
        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Check for curl errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$curl_errors"
        return 1
    fi
    rm -f "$curl_errors"

    # Check for API errors in response
    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        local error_msg
        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract content from response
    local content
    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')

    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
        log_error "Response: $output"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$content"
}

# Internal: Invoke Gemini CLI provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from Gemini CLI
_llm_invoke_gemini_cli() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local gemini_bin
    gemini_bin=$(llm_find_bin "gemini-cli")

    log_debug "Invoking Gemini CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Using Gemini binary: ${gemini_bin}"

    # Invoke Gemini CLI
    # NOTE: This is a placeholder implementation - actual Gemini CLI may differ
    local output
    local gemini_errors
    gemini_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(timeout "$timeout" "$gemini_bin" \
        --model "$model" \
        <<EOF
$prompt
EOF
    2>"$gemini_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$gemini_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Gemini CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$gemini_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$gemini_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Gemini CLI stderr: $(cat "$gemini_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$gemini_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Internal: Invoke custom command template provider
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds
# Returns:
#   Raw output from custom command
_llm_invoke_custom() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="$3"

    local custom_bin
    custom_bin=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN")

    if [ -z "$custom_bin" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_BIN not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    local command_template
    command_template=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND")

    if [ -z "$command_template" ]; then
        log_error "LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND not configured for custom provider"
        return 1
    fi

    log_debug "Invoking custom provider with model ${model} and timeout ${timeout}s"
    log_debug "Binary: ${custom_bin}"
    log_debug "Template: ${command_template}"

    # Write prompt to temporary file
    local prompt_file
    prompt_file=$(mktemp)
    echo "$prompt" > "$prompt_file"

    # Substitute placeholders in command template
    local command="$command_template"
    command="${command//\{BIN\}/$custom_bin}"
    command="${command//\{MODEL\}/$model}"
    command="${command//\{PROMPT_FILE\}/$prompt_file}"
    command="${command//\{TIMEOUT\}/$timeout}"

    log_debug "Executing: $command"

    # Execute command
    local output
    local command_errors
    command_errors=$(mktemp)

    set +e
    output=$(eval "$command" 2>"$command_errors")
    local exit_code=$?
    set -e

    # Cleanup prompt file
    rm -f "$prompt_file"

    # Check for errors
    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
        local errors
        errors=$(cat "$command_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_error "Custom command invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
        log_error "Command: $command"
        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
        rm -f "$command_errors"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for stderr warnings
    if [ -s "$command_errors" ]; then
        log_warn "Custom command stderr: $(cat "$command_errors")"
    fi
    rm -f "$command_errors"

    echo "$output"
}

# Invoke LLM with pluggable provider support
# Args:
#   $1 - model name
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
llm_invoke() {
    local model="$1"
    local prompt="$2"
    local timeout="${3:-30}"

    # Get provider from config
    local provider
    provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    log_debug "LLM invocation: provider=$provider, model=$model, timeout=${timeout}s"

    # Dispatch to provider-specific function
    local output
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_claude_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        openai-api)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_openai_api "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        gemini-cli)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_gemini_cli "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        custom)
            output=$(_llm_invoke_custom "$model" "$prompt" "$timeout") || return 1
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown LLM provider: $provider"
            log_error "Valid providers: claude-cli, openai-api, gemini-cli, custom"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac

    # Extract JSON from output (handle markdown wrapping)
    local json
    json=$(llm_extract_json "$output")

    # Validate JSON
    if ! json_validate "$json"; then
        log_error "LLM output is not valid JSON: $json"
        return 1
    fi

    echo "$json"
}

# DEPRECATED: Use llm_invoke() instead
# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
# Args:
#   $1 - model name (e.g., "haiku-4.5", "haiku-3.5")
#   $2 - prompt text
#   $3 - timeout in seconds (optional, default: 30)
# Returns:
#   JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
# Exits:
#   1 on error
claude_invoke() {
    # Backward compatibility wrapper - delegates to llm_invoke
    llm_invoke "$@"
}

# Extract JSON from LLM output (handles markdown wrapping)
# Args:
#   $1 - LLM output text
# Returns:
#   Extracted JSON
llm_extract_json() {
    local output="$1"

    # Try to extract from markdown code block first
    local extracted
    extracted=$(json_extract_from_markdown "$output")

    # If extraction changed the output, use it
    if [ "$extracted" != "$output" ]; then
        echo "$extracted"
        return 0
    fi

    # Otherwise, look for JSON object in output
    # Match from first { to last }
    if echo "$output" | grep -q '{'; then
        echo "$output" | sed -n '/{/,/}/p'
    else
        echo "$output"
    fi
}

# DEPRECATED: Use llm_extract_json() instead
# Backward compatibility alias
claude_extract_json() {
    llm_extract_json "$@"
}

# ============================================================================
# Sidekick Configuration Defaults
# ============================================================================
#
# This file contains all default configuration values for Sidekick.
# Values can be overridden in:
#   - User config: ~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#   - Project config: $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf
#
# Configuration cascade: this file → user → project (later wins)
#
# Format: Shell KEY=VALUE pairs (sourced by bash)

# ============================================================================
# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
# ============================================================================

# Topic extraction: LLM-based conversation analysis
FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Resume: Generate snarkified resume topic from previous session
FEATURE_RESUME=true

# Statusline: Enhanced statusline with topic and metrics
FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true

# Tracking: Request counting
FEATURE_TRACKING=true

# Reminder: Periodic static reminder injection (depends on tracking)
FEATURE_REMINDER=true

# Cleanup: Garbage collection for old session directories
FEATURE_CLEANUP=true

# ============================================================================
# LLM PROVIDER CONFIGURATION
# ============================================================================

# LLM provider selection
# Options: claude-cli | openai-api | gemini-cli | custom
LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api

# ============================================================================
# CLAUDE CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -haiku (4.5)  $1.00 / $5.00
# -sonnet (4.5) $3.00 / $15.00  (for in/out < 200k tokens)
# -opus (4.1)   $15.00 / $75
# ============================================================================

# Path to Claude CLI binary (auto-detect if empty)
# Searches: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
LLM_CLAUDE_BIN=

# Model name for Claude provider (used when LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli)
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku

# ============================================================================
# OPENAI API PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
#
# -gpt-5-nano  $0.05 / $0.40
# -gpt-o4-mini $0.15 / $0.60
# -gpt-5-mini  $0.25 / $2.00
# ============================================================================

# OpenAI API key (required when LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api)
# Can also be set via OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable
LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=

# OpenAI API endpoint
LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions

# Model name for OpenAI provider
# Options: gpt-o4-mini, gpt-5-mini, gpt-5-nano
LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-5-nano

# ============================================================================
# GEMINI CLI PROVIDER
#
# Pricing (input/output per million tokens):
# -gemini-2.5-flash-lite $0.10 / 0.40
# -gemini-2.5-flash      $0.30 / 2.50
# -gemini-2.5-pro        $1.25 / $10.00 (in/out tokens < 200k)
# ============================================================================

# Path to Gemini CLI binary
LLM_GEMINI_BIN=gemini

# Model name for Gemini provider
# Options: gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite, gemini-2.5-pro
LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash-lite

# ============================================================================
# CUSTOM PROVIDER
# ============================================================================

# Custom command template with placeholders:
#   {BIN} - binary path (from LLM_CUSTOM_BIN)
#   {MODEL} - model name (from LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL)
#   {PROMPT_FILE} - path to temporary file containing prompt
#   {TIMEOUT} - timeout in seconds
# Example: {BIN} --model {MODEL} --timeout {TIMEOUT} < {PROMPT_FILE}
LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND=

# Binary path for custom provider
LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=

# Model name for custom provider
LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=default

# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION
# ============================================================================

# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
# Model to use for topic extraction
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku

# Number of transcript lines to analyze (default: 80 ≈ 3-5 messages)
TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80

# Filter out tool_use and tool_result messages from analysis (reduces noise and tokens)
TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true

# Cadence for high clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10

# Cadence for low clarity conversations (responses between analyses)
TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1

# Clarity threshold (1-10 scale)
# Conversations with clarity >= threshold use high cadence
# Conversations with clarity < threshold use low cadence
TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7

# ============================================================================
# SLEEPER (Background Polling Process)
# ============================================================================

# Enable sleeper process
SLEEPER_ENABLED=true

# Maximum sleeper inactivity timeout (seconds)
# Sleeper will exit after this duration of no transcript activity
SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600

# Minimum transcript size change to trigger analysis (bytes)
SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500

# Minimum interval between analyses (seconds)
SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10

# Dynamic sleep interval (based on clarity score)
# Formula: clarity * 2 seconds (capped between min and max)
# - Low clarity (1-2): sleep 2-4 seconds (more frequent checks)
# - Medium clarity (5): sleep 10 seconds
# - High clarity (10): sleep 20 seconds (less frequent checks)
SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2
SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20

# ============================================================================
# RESUME (Session Continuity)
# ============================================================================

# DEPRECATED: Use LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL instead
# Kept for backward compatibility - will be removed in future version
# Model to use for resume snarkification
# Options: haiku, sonnet, opus
RESUME_MODEL=haiku

# Minimum clarity score to use previous session's topic
# Only sessions with clarity >= this value are considered for resume
RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5

# ============================================================================
# STATUSLINE
# ============================================================================

# Token budget threshold for percentage calculation
# Statusline will show percentage of this value
STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000

# ============================================================================
# TRACKING
# ============================================================================

# (No tracking-specific configuration at this time)

# ============================================================================
# REMINDER
# ============================================================================

# Static reminder cadence (responses)
# Reminder will be injected every N responses
TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4

# ============================================================================
# CLEANUP (Session Directory Garbage Collection)
# ============================================================================

# Enable automatic cleanup
# If disabled, old session directories will never be removed
CLEANUP_ENABLED=true

# Minimum number of old sessions before cleanup triggers
# Cleanup only runs when old session count >= this value
CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5

# Age threshold for old sessions (days)
# Sessions older than this are candidates for cleanup
CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2

# Dry run mode (log but don't delete)
# Useful for testing cleanup behavior
CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false

# ============================================================================
# GLOBAL
# ============================================================================

# Log level: debug | info | warn | error
# Controls verbosity of log output
#   debug: All messages (very verbose)
#   info: Informational and above
#   warn: Warnings and errors only
#   error: Errors only
LOG_LEVEL=info

# Override Claude CLI binary path (optional)
# If empty, will search: CLAUDE_BIN env → ~/.claude/local/claude → PATH
CLAUDE_BIN=

```bash
local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
```

This is setting `model` to the value of `TOPIC_MODEL`, which is "haiku" by default in config.defaults (line 124). 

Then line 210 checks:
```bash
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku-4.5" ]; then
```

This condition only enters the provider-specific model selection if:
1. `model` is empty, OR

Then line 72 checks if it's either empty OR exactly "haiku". So this one actually WORKS correctly because the default value is "haiku", which matches the condition.

However, topic-extraction.sh on line 210 checks:
```bash
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku-4.5" ]; then
```

But the default TOPIC_MODEL is "haiku", NOT "haiku-4.5", so this condition fails and it never enters the case statement to select the provider-specific model.

So the bug is:

2. This affects ALL providers - not just OpenAI

The fix is simple: change line 210 in topic-extraction.sh from:
```bash
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku-4.5" ]; then
```
to:
```bash
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku" ]; then
```

This matches the pattern used in generate-resume.sh (line 72) which correctly checks for "haiku".

content.text:
Perfect! I found the bug. It's in **topic-extraction.sh:210** - it's checking for the wrong default value.

**The Problem:**

In `topic-extraction.sh:209-210`:
```bash
local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku-4.5" ]; then
```

But in `config.defaults:124`, the default is:
```bash
TOPIC_MODEL=haiku
```

So the condition on line 210 fails because `$model` is "haiku", not "haiku-4.5". Therefore it **never enters the case statement** to select the provider-specific model (LLM_OPENAI_MODEL, LLM_GEMINI_MODEL, etc.).

**Contrast with generate-resume.sh** (which works correctly):
```bash
resume_model=$(config_get 'RESUME_MODEL')
if [ -z "$resume_model" ] || [ "$resume_model" = "haiku" ]; then
```

This correctly checks for "haiku", matching the default in config.defaults:176.

**Impact:**

This affects **ALL providers** (OpenAI, Gemini, Custom) - not just OpenAI. They all get "haiku" passed to their LLM invocation functions, which will obviously fail for non-Claude providers.

**The Fix:**

Change topic-extraction.sh:210 from:
```bash
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku-4.5" ]; then
```

to:
```bash
if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku" ]; then
```

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of preprocessed messages (configurable line count)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local line_count
    line_count=$(config_get "TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES")
    line_count=${line_count:-80}

    local filter_tools
    filter_tools=$(config_get "TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES")
    filter_tools=${filter_tools:-true}

    # Build jq filter for preprocessing
    local jq_filter='.message | select(. != null)'

    # Optionally filter out tool messages (tool_use and tool_result)
    if [ "$filter_tools" = "true" ]; then
        jq_filter+=' | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end))'
    fi

    # Strip unnecessary attributes to reduce token usage
    jq_filter+=' | del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)'

    log_debug "Extracting last $line_count lines with preprocessing (filter_tools=$filter_tools)"

    # Extract, preprocess, and wrap in array
    tail -n "$line_count" "$transcript_path" | jq -c "$jq_filter" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    # Support legacy TOPIC_MODEL config, but prefer provider-specific model config
    local model=$(config_get "TOPIC_MODEL")
    if [ -z "$model" ] || [ "$model" = "haiku-4.5" ]; then
        # Use provider-specific model (e.g., LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL)
        local provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
        provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

        case "$provider" in
            claude-cli)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
                model=${model:-haiku}
                ;;
            openai-api)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
                model=${model:-gpt-4-turbo}
                ;;
            gemini-cli)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_GEMINI_MODEL")
                model=${model:-gemini-pro}
                ;;
            custom)
                model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
                model=${model:-default}
                ;;
        esac
    fi

    log_debug "Topic analysis using model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Invoke LLM
    log_debug "Invoking LLM for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
        log_error "LLM invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/sleeper-loop.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Sleeper loop script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/generate-resume.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Resume generation script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$session_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for topic extraction
#
# Ensures the sleeper process is running (watchdog pattern)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Topic Extraction: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Ensure sleeper is running (watchdog pattern)
    # sleeper_start checks if already running and returns early if so
    if ! topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
        log_warn "Failed to ensure sleeper is running (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Topic extraction feature loaded"

1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# llm.sh - LLM invocation namespace for Sidekick hooks system
     3→#
     4→# Provides pluggable LLM provider system with support for:
     5→#   - Claude CLI (default)
     6→#   - OpenAI API
     7→#   - Gemini CLI
     8→#   - Custom command templates
     9→#
    10→# Functions:
    11→#   - llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - Invoke configured LLM provider
    12→#   - llm_find_bin(provider) - Find binary for provider
    13→#   - llm_extract_json(output) - Extract JSON from LLM output
    14→#   - claude_invoke(model, prompt, timeout) - DEPRECATED: Use llm_invoke
    15→#   - claude_extract_json(output) - DEPRECATED: Use llm_extract_json

    17→
    18→# Double-source guard
    19→if [ -n "${_SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED:-}" ]; then

    22→readonly _SIDEKICK_LLM_LOADED=1
    23→
    24→
    25→# LLM INVOCATION NAMESPACE
    26→# ============================================================================
    27→# Pluggable LLM provider system supporting multiple backends:
    28→#   - claude-cli: Claude Code CLI (current default)
    29→#   - openai-api: OpenAI API via curl
    30→#   - gemini-cli: Google Gemini CLI
    31→#   - custom: User-defined command template
    32→#
    33→# Provider selection via LLM_PROVIDER config variable
    34→# ============================================================================
    35→
    36→# Find Claude CLI binary
    37→# DEPRECATED: Use llm_find_bin("claude-cli") instead
    38→# Returns:
    39→#   Path to claude binary
    40→# Exits:
    41→#   3 if claude binary not found
    42→claude_find_bin() {

    45→        echo "$CLAUDE_BIN"

    48→
    49→    # 2. Check ~/.claude/local/claude (default installation)
    50→    local local_claude="${HOME}/.claude/local/claude"
    51→    if [ -x "$local_claude" ]; then
    52→        echo "$local_claude"

    64→    # Not found

    69→
    70→# Find LLM binary for specified provider
    71→# Args:
    72→#   $1 - provider name (claude-cli, gemini-cli, custom)
    73→# Returns:
    74→#   Path to binary
    75→# Exits:
    76→#   3 if binary not found
    77→llm_find_bin() {
    78→    local provider="$1"
    79→    local bin_path=""
    80→    local config_var=""
    81→    local default_name=""
    82→
    83→    case "$provider" in

   105→            ;;

   138→
   139→# Internal: Invoke Claude CLI provider
   140→# Args:
   141→#   $1 - model name
   142→#   $2 - prompt text
   143→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   144→# Returns:
   145→#   Raw output from Claude CLI
   146→_llm_invoke_claude_cli() {
   147→    local model="$1"
   148→    local prompt="$2"
   149→    local timeout="$3"
   150→
   151→    local claude_bin
   152→    claude_bin=$(llm_find_bin "claude-cli")
   153→
   154→    # Create isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   155→    local workspace
   156→    workspace=$(workspace_create "claude-invoke-$$")
   157→
   158→    log_debug "Invoking Claude CLI model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s in workspace ${workspace}"
   159→    log_debug "Using Claude binary: ${claude_bin}"
   160→
   161→    # Invoke Claude using project settings in isolated workspace
   162→    local output
   163→    local claude_errors
   164→    local claude_output_file
   165→    claude_errors=$(mktemp)
   166→    claude_output_file=$(mktemp)
   167→
   168→    # Use heredoc to pass prompt, write to temp file to avoid subshell issues
   169→    # Disable exit-on-error temporarily to capture exit code
   170→    set +e
   171→    (cd "$workspace" && timeout "$timeout" "$claude_bin" -p \
   172→        --model "$model" \
   173→        --setting-sources project \
   174→        <<EOF
   175→$prompt
   176→EOF
   177→    ) > "$claude_output_file" 2>"$claude_errors"
   178→    local exit_code=$?
   179→    set -e
   180→
   181→    # Read output from file
   182→    output=$(cat "$claude_output_file")
   183→    rm -f "$claude_output_file"
   184→
   185→    # Check if Claude invocation failed
   186→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   187→        local errors
   188→        errors=$(cat "$claude_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   189→        log_error "Claude CLI invocation failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   190→        log_error "Command: cd $workspace && $claude_bin -p --model $model --setting-sources project"
   191→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   192→        log_error "Stdout: ${output}"
   193→        rm -f "$claude_errors"
   194→        workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   195→        return 1
   196→    fi
   197→
   198→    # Check for stderr warnings
   199→    if [ -s "$claude_errors" ]; then
   200→        log_warn "Claude CLI stderr: $(cat "$claude_errors")"
   201→    fi
   202→    rm -f "$claude_errors"
   203→
   204→    # Cleanup workspace
   205→    workspace_cleanup "$workspace"
   206→
   207→    echo "$output"
   208→}
   209→
   210→# Internal: Invoke OpenAI API provider
   211→# Args:
   212→#   $1 - model name
   213→#   $2 - prompt text
   214→#   $3 - timeout in seconds
   215→# Returns:
   216→#   Raw JSON output from OpenAI API
   217→_llm_invoke_openai_api() {
   218→    local model="$1"
   219→    local prompt="$2"
   220→    local timeout="$3"
   221→
   222→    # Get API key from config or environment
   223→    local api_key
   224→    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
   225→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   226→        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
   227→    fi
   228→
   229→    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
   230→        log_error "OpenAI API key not found. Set LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY in config or OPENAI_API_KEY env var"
   231→        return 1
   232→    fi
   233→
   234→    # Get endpoint
   235→    local endpoint
   236→    endpoint=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT")
   237→    endpoint=${endpoint:-https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions}
   238→
   239→    log_debug "Invoking OpenAI API model ${model} with timeout ${timeout}s"
   240→    log_debug "Using endpoint: ${endpoint}"
   241→
   242→    # Build JSON payload (escape quotes in prompt)
   243→    local escaped_prompt
   244→    escaped_prompt=$(echo "$prompt" | jq -Rs .)
   245→
   246→    local payload
   247→    payload=$(cat <<EOF
   248→{
   249→  "model": "$model",
   250→  "messages": [
   251→    {
   252→      "role": "user",
   253→      "content": $escaped_prompt
   254→    }
   255→  ],
   256→  "temperature": 0.7
   257→}
   258→EOF
   259→)
   260→
   261→    # Invoke API with curl
   262→    local output
   263→    local curl_errors
   264→    curl_errors=$(mktemp)
   265→
   266→    set +e
   267→    output=$(curl -s --max-time "$timeout" \
   268→        -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
   269→        -H "Authorization: Bearer $api_key" \
   270→        -d "$payload" \
   271→        "$endpoint" 2>"$curl_errors")
   272→    local exit_code=$?
   273→    set -e
   274→
   275→    # Check for curl errors
   276→    if [ $exit_code -ne 0 ]; then
   277→        local errors
   278→        errors=$(cat "$curl_errors" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
   279→        log_error "OpenAI API call failed (exit code: ${exit_code})"
   280→        log_error "Stderr: ${errors}"
   281→        rm -f "$curl_errors"
   282→        return 1
   283→    fi
   284→    rm -f "$curl_errors"
   285→
   286→    # Check for API errors in response
   287→    if echo "$output" | jq -e '.error' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   288→        local error_msg
   289→        error_msg=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.error.message // .error')
   290→        log_error "OpenAI API returned error: ${error_msg}"
   291→        return 1
   292→    fi
   293→
   294→    # Extract content from response
   295→    local content
   296→    content=$(echo "$output" | jq -r '.choices[0].message.content // empty')
   297→
   298→    if [ -z "$content" ]; then
   299→        log_error "Failed to extract content from OpenAI response"
   300→        log_error "Response: $output"
   301→        return 1
   302→    fi
   303→
   304→    echo "$content"
   305→}
   306→
   307→# Internal: Invoke Gemini CLI provider
   308→# Args:
   309→#   $1 - model name
   310→#   $2 - prompt text
